title: Does distance matter in spillover?<sup>1</sup>
abstract: Abstract Our aim in this paper is twofold: to find whether FDI causes horizontal or vertical productivity spillovers to domestically‐owned Hungarian manufacturing firms, and to see if distance matters in spillovers. For this exercise we use a large panel of Hungarian firms and different panel models. Consistently with previous research, at the country level, we find positive vertical spillovers but no evidence of positive horizontal spillovers. By taking distance into consideration, however, we find positive horizontal spillovers for domestic firms close to foreign‐owned firms. By constructing spillover measures weighted by distance, we find similar patterns. Our results underline the importance of labour market rigidity and the local nature of knowledge in the case of horizontal spillovers.
summary_en: Foreign direct investment is supposed to generate spillovers to domestic firms — so why do empirical tests often fail to find them? This paper argues distance is the missing variable. Using Hungarian manufacturing data, the authors confirm the usual finding: at the country level, FDI raises productivity of domestic suppliers and buyers ("vertical") but not of competitors ("horizontal"). However, when distance between firms is factored in, they find positive horizontal spillovers for domestic firms located close to foreign-owned ones. Spillovers are local, not national — labour-market rigidities and place-based knowledge networks are the likely channel.
data_used: Hungarian firm-level panel from APEH/NAV corporate register, 1996–2003; manufacturing; full population of firms; combined with firm-location data to compute pairwise distances.
policy_relevance: Direct Hungarian-policy relevance. For HIPA and regional-development policy: FDI's benefits to domestic firms are more localised than commonly assumed. Targeting FDI to regions where domestic firm clusters already exist should deliver larger spillovers than spreading it thin. Also suggests that domestic-firm support should pair with FDI attraction in the same location. Relevant for Ministry of Economy, regional-development agencies, and EU cohesion-fund allocation.
